graph_vector_fulltext.message:
The prohibitions on unacceptable AI practices (and the general provisions) apply from **2 February 2025**.  
The Regulation applies in full from **2 August 2026**.
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_question: From what date do the prohibitions on unacceptable AI practices apply, and from what date does the Regulation apply in full?
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_answer:
The prohibitions on unacceptable AI practices (and the general provisions) apply from **2 February 2025**.  
The Regulation applies in full from **2 August 2026**.
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_contexts:

Content: Text Content:
 for the implementation of the relevant obligations, if, by the time this Regulation becomes applicable,  a code of practice cannot be finalised or is not deemed adequate by the AI Office. Once a harmonised standard is  EN OJ L, 12.7.2024 30/144 ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj
----
 apply from 2 August 2026. However, taking into account the unacceptable risk associated  with the use of AI in certain ways, the prohibitions as well as the general provisions of this Regulation should already  apply from 2 February 2025. While the full effect of those prohibitions follows with the establishment of the  governance and enforcement of this Regulation, anticipating the application of the prohibitions is important to take  account of unacceptable risks and to have an effect on other procedures, such as in civil law. Moreover, the
----
-purpose AI models should apply from 2 August 2025. Codes of practice should be ready by  2 May 2025 in view of enabling providers to demonstrate compliance on time. The AI Office should ensure that  classification rules and procedures are up to date in light of technological developments. In addition, Member States  should lay down and notify to the Commission the rules on penalties, including administrative fines, and ensure that  they are properly and effectively implemented by the date of application of this Regulation. Therefore the provisions 
----
REGULATION (EU) 2024/1689 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL of 13 June 2024 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence and amending Regulations (EC) No 300/2008,  (EU) No 167/2013, (EU) No 168/2013, (EU) 2018/858, (EU) 2018/1139 and (EU) 2019/2144 and  Directives 2014/90/EU,
----

chunks.text: for the implementation of the relevant obligations, if, by the time this Regulation becomes applicable, a code of practice cannot be finalised or is not deemed adequate by the AI Office. Once a harmonised standard is EN OJ L, 12.7.2024 30/144 ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj

apply from 2 August 2026. However, taking into account the unacceptable risk associated with the use of AI in certain ways, the prohibitions as well as the general provisions of this Regulation should already apply from 2 February 2025. While the full effect of those prohibitions follows with the establishment of the governance and enforcement of this Regulation, anticipating the application of the prohibitions is important to take account of unacceptable risks and to have an effect on other procedures, such as in civil law. Moreover, the

-purpose AI models should apply from 2 August 2025. Codes of practice should be ready by 2 May 2025 in view of enabling providers to demonstrate compliance on time. The AI Office should ensure that classification rules and procedures are up to date in light of technological developments. In addition, Member States should lay down and notify to the Commission the rules on penalties, including administrative fines, and ensure that they are properly and effectively implemented by the date of application of this Regulation. Therefore the provisions
